title: Copilot Studio AI Agents for Security & Operations
summary: Deployed custom AI agents in Copilot Studio for service desk triage, security alert classification, QA automation, and policy lookup. Each agent operates under HIPAA-aligned governance — Purview DLP on prompts, Conditional Access on agent surfaces, full audit logging through Sentinel.
problem: AI adoption across the organization was happening organically — staff were pasting clinical and operational content into public LLM tools with no governance, no audit trail, and no awareness of the data exposure they were creating. The CISO needed to lead the AI conversation rather than react to it, which meant shipping sanctioned agents fast enough to compete with shadow tooling.
approach: Inventoried existing shadow AI usage by surveying staff anonymously and reviewing egress patterns to public LLM endpoints
Defined the governance model first — Purview DLP scoped to AI prompt surfaces, Conditional Access policies on Copilot endpoints, audit logging into Sentinel — before building a single agent
Built service desk triage agent that classifies tickets and routes them to the right queue with confidence scoring
Built security alert pre-processing agent that summarizes Sentinel incidents with context before analyst review
Built QA automation agent for contract documentation review against compliance checklists
Built policy lookup agent that lets staff query internal policy by intent rather than navigating SharePoint trees
Trained leadership and end users on what the sanctioned agents do, where shadow AI is unacceptable, and the fast-track approval path for new use cases
lessons: AI governance is data governance with extra steps. The hard part isn't the agent — Copilot Studio gives you most of an agent in an afternoon — it's defining what data the agent can see, what prompts it can take, and where its outputs can land. Build that boundary in Purview and Conditional Access first, then build the agent against it. Also: ship sanctioned tools faster than staff can adopt shadow tools. The fastest way to lose the AI governance conversation is to take six months reviewing while your users are already pasting PHI into public chatbots.
